text: Abstract Background: Incidence of IFIs has increased all over the globe in recent years mainly because of the increasing incidence of immunocompromised conditions. So the diagnosis at the initial stages is crucial and for this the diagnostic process has to be done in a most effective and rapid manner. It has to be remembered that time is of essence when it comes to management of a IFI. Moreover the advancements in the field of clinical mycology have made way for many rapid and more reliable and sensitive detection methods and have changed the face of clinical mycology in such a way that the classical and more traditional methods such as the use of microscope and culture medium etc are considered to be old-fashioned. Also the molecular techniques like PCR and other associated techniques like genotypic tests, probe-based hybridization system like T2 Candida, microfluidic chip-based system and the next generation sequencing, biosensors of new generation along with nanotechnology are making the fungal diagnosis a easy and simpler task with very good accuracy and with less time consumption. Here we have put up the progress in all these latest diagnostic technologies in a most ordered manner. Main text: The whole information required for this review was done through an extensive literature survey carried out by the group of researchers including the collection and extraction of data; detailed information analysis of all the related literature; compiling of various information contained in all the sub-sections required to prepare this comprehensive review. This review paper highlights the latest progresses and advances in molecular methods as well as in the conventional methods such as serology-based methods with recent advancements and also about the advancements in the biosensor technology along with the artificial intelligence-based models which were not elaborated or reviewed at least till now in the context of IFIs and hence this paper in an endeavour to do so to the best of our knowledge. Conclusion: This review will definitely aid the scientific community to update their knowledge regarding various latest advancements which could be used as supplementary tools in laboratory diagnostic processes to diagnose an IFI at an early stage in an immunocompromised patient.
